title: Overriding Methods ⚔️
description: How a subclass can rebel and change the behavior it inherited.

slides.title: Changing the Rules ⚔️
slides.content:
# The `override` keyword

A Subclass generally inherits everything its Parent does. 

But what if we inherit a `.attack()` method that does 10 damage, but our new `BossEnemy` class needs its attack to deal 100 damage instead?

We can completely replace the parent's function using the `override` keyword!

slides.title: Override Syntax

quizData.question: What is the primary function of the `override` keyword?

options.text: To safely provide a brand new, custom implementation of a Method that was inherited from a Parent class.

quizData.explanation: Overriding lets you keep the inherited structure (like having an `attack` method) but completely change the underlying logic for that specific Subclass.

slides.title: The 'super' Keyword 🦸‍♂️

quizData.question: What does calling `super.run()` do inside an overridden method?

options.text: It runs the original code defined in the Parent (Superclass).
It makes the app run faster.

quizData.explanation: The keyword `super` directly points to the Superclass (Parent). It allows you to trigger their code safely before executing your own.
